originalAlarm.description: The value written to `&pid_output` is never used.

presenceCondition: (and (= MARLIN_H false) (= PIDTEMP true) (= PID_DEBUG true))
model:
(define-fun PID_DEBUG () Bool
  true)
(define-fun MARLIN_H () Bool
  false)
(define-fun PIDTEMP () Bool
  true)
reasoning: L11: `float pid_output = 1` in manage_heater(). When PIDTEMP=T, the else branch (L19) is skipped, and pid_output is never used before function return — dead store. This dead store exists whenever PIDTEMP=T, regardless of PID_DEBUG or MARLIN_H. No '// ERROR' at L11 → new bug not in VBDB (ERROR at L16 is for SERIAL_ECHO_START, a different issue). PC requires MARLIN_H=F and PID_DEBUG=T — MARLIN_H=F is only needed to compile SERIAL_ECHO_START when PID_DEBUG=T, but the dead store also occurs when PID_DEBUG=F (any MARLIN_H). PC is over-constrained. Classification: TRUE_NEW_OVER.
